title: Getting Started with oncoAgent
text: oncoAgent semi-autonomous research system oncology target discovery integrates multi-omics data agentic AI workflows.

title: Getting Started with oncoAgent
code: library(oncoAgent)  config <- create_pipeline_config(   project = "TCGA-BRCA",   cancer_type = "breast cancer",   de_methods = c("deseq2", "edger", "limma"),   padj_threshold = 0.05,   output_dir = "results" )

title: Getting Started with oncoAgent
code: # Using synthetic/demo data counts <- matrix(rnbinom(100 * 20, mu = 100, size = 10),                  nrow = 100, ncol = 20) rownames(counts) <- paste0("Gene_", 1:100) colnames(counts) <- paste0("Sample_", 1:20)  col_data <- data.frame(   condition = factor(rep(c("tumor", "normal"), each = 10)),   row.names = colnames(counts) )  clinical <- data.frame(   sample_id = colnames(counts),   time = rexp(20, 0.01),   status = rbinom(20, 1, 0.5) )  result <- run_oncopipeline(   config,   existing_data = list(     expression = counts,     col_data = col_data,     clinical = clinical   ) )

title: Getting Started with oncoAgent
code: config <- create_pipeline_config(   project = "TCGA-BRCA",   cancer_type = "breast cancer" )  result <- run_oncopipeline(config)

title: Getting Started with oncoAgent
code: # Fetch TCGA data tcga <- fetch_tcga_data("TCGA-BRCA")  # Fetch DepMap CRISPR data crispr <- fetch_depmap_crispr()  # Fetch drug sensitivity gdsc <- fetch_gdsc_drug_sensitivity()

title: Getting Started with oncoAgent
code: # Single method de_deseq2 <- run_deseq2(counts, col_data)  # Consensus across methods de_consensus <- consensus_de(counts, col_data,                              methods = c("deseq2", "edger", "limma"))

title: Getting Started with oncoAgent
code: hr_table <- calculate_hazard_ratios(   counts,   time = clinical$time,   status = clinical$status )

title: Getting Started with oncoAgent
code: genes <- rownames(counts)[1:50] ppi <- fetch_string_ppi(genes) network <- build_ppi_network(ppi) centrality <- calculate_network_centrality(network) modules <- detect_network_modules(network)

title: Getting Started with oncoAgent
code: scores <- composite_target_score(   de_results = de_consensus,   survival_hr = hr_table,   centrality = centrality )  ranked <- rank_targets(scores, top_n = 20)

title: Getting Started with oncoAgent
text: five agents work together:
code: # Data Discovery Agent dd_agent <- DataDiscoveryAgent$new(project = "TCGA-BRCA") dd_agent$assess(existing_data)  # Analysis Agent analysis_agent <- AnalysisAgent$new() results <- analysis_agent$run(data)  # Gap Detection Agent gap_agent <- GapDetectionAgent$new() gaps <- gap_agent$detect(results, data)  # Hypothesis Agent hyp_agent <- HypothesisAgent$new() hypotheses <- hyp_agent$generate(results$scoring)  # Evaluation Agent eval_agent <- EvaluationAgent$new() metrics <- eval_agent$evaluate(ranked, gold_standard)

title: Getting Started with oncoAgent
code: gs <- build_reference_panel(sources = c("cosmic", "cgc"))  metrics <- benchmark_vs_goldstandard(ranked, gs) auroc <- calculate_auroc(ranked, gs$genes) report <- generate_evaluation_report(ranked, gs, "eval_report.md")

what: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery from Multi-Omics Data
title: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery from Multi-Omics Data
text: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery Multi-Omics Data

title: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery from Multi-Omics Data
text: oncoAgent semi-autonomous research system integrates multi-omics data (genomics, transcriptomics, CRISPR dependency screens, drug sensitivity) protein interaction networks clinical outcomes identify prioritize oncology drug targets. system features five specialized AI agents collaborate adaptive workflows: detecting data gaps, suggesting next analyses, generating hypotheses, benchmarking predictions gold-standard cancer gene sets (COSMIC, Cancer Gene Census). Unlike static pipelines, oncoAgent iteratively refines analysis evidence quality meets predefined thresholds.

title: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery from Multi-Omics Data
text: oncoAgent builds established methods following peer-reviewed work:

title: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery from Multi-Omics Data
text: Cancer Genome Atlas (TCGA): Pan-cancer genomic characterization resource spanning 33 cancer types, 11,000+ tumors 12. oncoAgent uses TCGAbiolinks programmatic access 3. Cancer Dependency Map (DepMap): Genome-scale CRISPR-Cas9 essentiality screens across 1,000+ cancer cell lines 45. Used identify genes selective dependencies specific cancer contexts. Genomics Drug Sensitivity Cancer (GDSC): Systematic drug response profiling linking genomic features pharmacological sensitivity 67. STRING Database: Protein-protein interaction network covering >14,000 organisms scored evidence experiments, databases, text mining 8. COSMIC Cancer Gene Census: Curated list genes mutations causally implicated cancer, used primary gold standard benchmarking 9.

title: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery from Multi-Omics Data
text: Differential Expression: Consensus ranking across DESeq2 10, edgeR 11, limma-voom 12, following best practices systematic benchmarking studies 13. Survival Analysis: Cox proportional hazards regression 14 LASSO-penalized variable selection multivariate models 15. Network Analysis: Louvain community detection PPI networks 16 centrality-based target prioritization, following network proximity paradigm 17. Target Scoring: Composite scoring integrating differential expression significance, survival association, network centrality, CRISPR dependency, Bayesian posterior ranking 18.

title: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery from Multi-Omics Data
code: # Install Bioconductor dependencies first if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))   install.packages("BiocManager") BiocManager::install(c("DESeq2", "edgeR", "limma", "SummarizedExperiment",                         "biomaRt", "pROC"))  # Install CRAN dependencies install.packages(c("survival", "glmnet", "igraph", "data.table",                     "dplyr", "tidyr", "purrr", "stringr", "jsonlite",                     "httr", "xml2", "ggplot2", "R6", "parallel"))  # Install oncoAgent from local source # R CMD INSTALL oncoAgent

title: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery from Multi-Omics Data
code: library(oncoAgent)  config <- create_pipeline_config(   project = "TCGA-BRCA",   cancer_type = "breast cancer",   de_methods = c("deseq2", "edger", "limma"),   padj_threshold = 0.05,   output_dir = "results" )

title: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery from Multi-Omics Data
code: set.seed(42)  # Simulate RNA-seq count matrix (1000 genes x 40 samples) n_genes <- 1000 n_samples <- 40  counts <- matrix(   rnbinom(n_genes * n_samples, mu = 200, size = 10),   nrow = n_genes, ncol = n_samples ) rownames(counts) <- paste0("Gene_", seq_len(n_genes)) colnames(counts) <- paste0("Sample_", seq_len(n_samples))  # Inject signal in first 50 genes (upregulated in tumor) counts[1:50, 1:20] <- rnbinom(50 * 20, mu = 1000, size = 10)  # Sample metadata col_data <- data.frame(   condition = factor(rep(c("tumor", "normal"), each = 20)),   row.names = colnames(counts) )  # Clinical data clinical <- data.frame(   sample_id = colnames(counts),   time = rexp(n_samples, rate = 0.002),   status = rbinom(n_samples, 1, 0.4),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  # Run full pipeline result <- run_oncopipeline(   config,   existing_data = list(     expression = counts,     col_data = col_data,     clinical = clinical   ) )  # View top targets head(result$targets[, c("gene", "composite_score", "target_tier")], 10)

title: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery from Multi-Omics Data
code: de <- consensus_de(counts, col_data,                    methods = c("deseq2", "edger", "limma"),                    padj_threshold = 0.05)  # Top DE genes detected by >= 2 methods head(de[de$n_methods >= 2, ], 10)

title: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery from Multi-Omics Data
code: # Univariate Cox regression per gene hr_table <- calculate_hazard_ratios(   counts,   time = clinical$time,   status = clinical$status,   genes = rownames(counts)[1:100] )  # Kaplan-Meier for a specific gene km <- run_kaplan_meier(   counts["Gene_1", ], clinical$time, clinical$status,   gene_name = "Gene_1", split_method = "median" )

title: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery from Multi-Omics Data
code: # Build PPI from synthetic edges (in practice, fetch from STRING) ppi <- data.frame(   from = sample(rownames(counts)[1:100], 200, replace = TRUE),   to = sample(rownames(counts)[1:100], 200, replace = TRUE),   score = sample(400:900, 200, replace = TRUE),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  g <- build_ppi_network(ppi, score_threshold = 400) centrality <- calculate_network_centrality(g) modules <- detect_network_modules(g)

title: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery from Multi-Omics Data
code: scores <- composite_target_score(   de_results = de,   survival_hr = hr_table,   centrality = centrality )  ranked <- rank_targets(scores, top_n = 20) print(ranked[, c("gene", "composite_score", "target_tier")])

title: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery from Multi-Omics Data
code: gs <- build_reference_panel(sources = c("cosmic", "cgc"))  eval_agent <- EvaluationAgent$new() metrics <- eval_agent$evaluate(ranked, gs) cat(eval_agent$report())

title: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery from Multi-Omics Data
text: oncoAgent implements five R6 agent classes collaborate adaptive loop:
code: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │                    Orchestration Layer                      │ │              run_oncopipeline(config)                       │ └──────────┬──────────┬─────────┬─────────┬───────────┬───────┘            │          │         │         │           |      ┌─────▼──┐ ┌─────▼──┐ ┌────▼───┐ ┌───▼────┐ ┌────▼─────┐      │ Data   │ │Analy-  │ │Hypo-   │ │ Gap    │ │Evalua-   │      │ Discov │ │sis     │ │thesis  │ │Detect  │ │tion      │      │ Agent  │ │Agent   │ │Agent   │ │Agent   │ │Agent     │      └────────┘ └────────┘ └────────┘ └────────┘ └──────────┘          │           │          │          │           │     Fetch data   Run DE/     Generate   Find gaps   Benchmark     Check gaps   Surv/Net    mechan-    Suggest     vs COSMIC                  pipelines   isms       fixes       CGC gold                                                     standards

title: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery from Multi-Omics Data
text: gap detection agent identifies: Missing data modalities (expression, clinical, PPI, CRISPR) Insufficient sample sizes (< 50 samples) Low DEG signal (< 100 significant genes) Conflicting signals scoring dimensions
code: # The agentic loop automatically re-runs if critical gaps are found adaptive_result <- adaptive_pipeline_loop(   config,   max_iterations = 5,   improvement_threshold = 0.01 )

title: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery from Multi-Omics Data
code: oncoAgent/ ├── R/ │   ├── data_ingestion.R         # TCGA, DepMap, GDSC, STRING, PubMed │   ├── preprocessing.R          # Normalization, batch correction, QC │   ├── differential_expression.R # DESeq2, edgeR, limma-voom, consensus │   ├── survival_analysis.R      # Cox PH, KM, LASSO-survival │   ├── network_analysis.R       # PPI, Louvain, centrality │   ├── target_scoring.R         # Composite, Bayesian, druggability │   ├── agents.R                 # 5 R6 agent classes │   ├── orchestration.R          # Pipeline runner, adaptive loop │   ├── evaluation.R             # AUROC, precision@K, benchmarking │   └── utils.R                  # Config, version ├── tests/testthat/              # 135 unit tests ├── vignettes/                   # Getting started tutorial ├── _targets.R                   # Reproducible targets pipeline ├── DESCRIPTION └── NAMESPACE

title: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery from Multi-Omics Data
text: complete end--end demo included inst/scripts/run_demo.R. : Builds reference panel COSMIC Cancer Gene Census Fetches PPI data STRING (synthetic fallback) Simulates realistic TCGA-BRCA-like RNA-seq data Runs consensus DE, survival analysis, network analysis Scores ranks targets Benchmarks gold standards Generates gap reports hypotheses Runtime: ~2-5 minutes.

what: oncoAgent: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery from Multi-Omics Data — oncoAgent-package
title: oncoAgent: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery from Multi-Omics Data — oncoAgent-package
text: semi-autonomous research system integrates multi-omics data (genomics, transcriptomics, CRISPR screens, drug sensitivity) literature mining clinical outcomes identify prioritize oncology drug targets. Features adaptive agentic workflows gap detection, hypothesis generation, benchmarking known cancer gene sets (COSMIC, Open Targets). Designed industry-grade target discovery pipelines pharma scale.

title: oncoAgent: Agentic AI-Driven Oncology Target Discovery from Multi-Omics Data — oncoAgent-package
text: Maintainer: Dany Mukesha danymukesha@gmail.com (ORCID)
